original_review:
I’ve been using Spotify since 2020 and used to love it. I used to be very adamant about never changing music apps, but I’m at my limit. 30 seconds used to be LONG for ad breaks, but now they’ve been reaching two minutes. I also used to be able to listen to 5-7 songs straight without ads, but now it’s more 3-4–5 if I’m REALLY lucky. 

Music is one of my biggest coping mechanisms, however I cant even have 15 minutes to myself without an ad. In fact, recently, they’ve changed it so ads will play right after the first song when you decide to start playing music. Not to mention the additional non-audio ads that pop up around the app as well. 

Now, the contents of these ads is another topic entirely. I’m not a purist by any means, but I really don’t enjoy hearing ads sampling a suggestive piece of a smutty audiobook, ESPECIALLY when I’m with family. 

If I hate the ads so much, why don’t I just buy premium? I used to, but then the price rose. And again, and again, and again. It is now THIRTEEN U.S. DOLLARS (fine, $12.99) for a single person. That is ridiculous. 

Additionally, the yearly music wraps have significantly decreased in quality. They used to be fun and accurate, but now parts are very obviously incorrect, nor do they seem to capture activity from the entire year. This is a big disappointment because, as someone who prides themself in listening to a wide range of genres, I loved seeing which ones I had explored each year. However, I began to see less appear in my “wraps” despite knowing very well I had listened to more. Whether it’s due to Spotify shrinking/simplifying genre groups or a lack of effort, I may never know, but it’s frustrating regardless.

Originally I had contemplated one star, but the fact that Spotify does offer a free listening experience grants it another. Nowadays it feels like you have to give an arm and a leg just for the bare minimum, but thankfully this wide range of music isn’t behind a paywall… just yet.
